summary: Nice soundboard capture with standout They Love Each Other and a smoking Scarlet > Fire, plus a strong Black Peter despite a slightly off‑beat Sugar Magnolia transition.
review: The recording delivers clear, well‑balanced sound that lets Phil’s bass on “They Love Each Other” shine and showcases Brent’s trippy keyboard textures of the ’83‑84 era. Musically the show is highlighted by a powerful “Scarlet > Fire” sequence in the second set and an early burst of “Black Peter,” though the segue into “Sugar Magnolia” feels a bit loose. Overall it’s an enjoyable performance that rewards repeated listening.

ai_rating.rationale: All four reviewers gave 4‑5 stars and praised both the musical moments and the sound quality, yielding a strong combined rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Very nice, clear sound with distinct ’83‑84 tonal character; no major dropouts or muddiness reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Second set solid with a smoking Scarlet > Fire; first set features an early Black Peter lead‑in that feels a bit rushed into Sugar Magnolia, creating a minor timing hiccup.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s opening licks to Black Peter are audible before the end of Throwing Stones, showing his eagerness and clear tone.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass work on “They Love Each Other” is highlighted and praised by reviewers.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s keyboards add a distinct, trippy texture characteristic of the 1983‑84 period.
